name: Brent Crude
summary: Day 79 of Hormuz blockade (Sunday). **MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICES ARE FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSES: BRENT $109.26 / WTI $105.42 / TTF Gas €50.85.** Energy futures (CME WTI, ICE Brent) resume Sunday evening US time (Monday 04:30 IST). **MONDAY MAY 18 CLOSE ABOVE $108 = 2-SESSION-SUSTAIN RULE MET = STANCHART $130-145 PATH ACTIVATES.** Per yfinance closes (BZ=F): May 12 $107.77 / May 13 $105.63 / May 14 $105.72 / May 15 $109.26 — only May 15 is a confirmed close above $108. **Monday May 18 close is the binary test**: above $108 = rule met → StanChart $130-145 path activates → cascade to inr-usd, nifty-50, sp-500, marine-war-risk-insurance. Below $108 = trinary $100-110 band holds against the strongest escalation signal of the cycle. **NEW WEEKEND OVERLAY**: NYT reported Israel + US 'most intense preparations yet to renew attacks on Iran possibly as early as next week' (REPORTED) including commando operation for nuclear material and Kharg Island ground option; Trump posted 'Calm Before the Storm' Truth Social image (CONFIRMED RHETORIC); Iran REPORTEDLY moved additional air defenses to Kharg Island; Pakistan-Naqvi mediation trip to Tehran simultaneously running (CONFIRMED ACTION). **REVERSIBILITY ASYMMETRIC**: Iran has 2 months under Trump letter. If Shamkhani-frame materializes (sanctions-for-dismantling), Brent gaps -$8-12 to $93-97 within 5 sessions. If Iran rejects formally and NYT preparation operationalizes into kinetic action, Brent gaps +$8-15 toward $130-145. **PRIOR (May 16):** BRENT $109.26 +3.3% 1D / +9.9% 1M / +62.1% 3M; WTI $105.42 +4.2% 1D / +11.3% 1M / +69.1% 3M (WTI booked +11% weekly gain — cleanest week of physical-tightness reflection in cycle)**. **PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION RESOLVED VIOLENTLY INTO PHYSICAL CONFIRMATION on May 15**: yesterday's paper-Brent $107.42 close + crisis-trade unwind has reversed cleanly — paper-equity capitulated, yields surged, oil ripped. **First close above $108 since the trigger was added — May 15 $109.26 (single-session). Per yfinance closes, May 12 $107.77, May 13 $105.63, May 14 $105.72 were all below $108; the 2-session-of-closes sustain rule is NOT yet met. Trigger stays WATCHING; StanChart $130-145 path stays DORMANT pending Monday May 18 close.** Primary chain (~85% crisis-linked): (1) **Trump-Xi summit produced joint statement only, no HEU-custodian operational deliverable**; (2) **Trump immediately reverted to escalatory rhetoric via Fox/Hannity/Truth Social** ('decimation to be continued' + 'not patient'); (3) **Trump on May 16 sent Iran a new nuclear ultimatum letter demanding full dismantling + halt all enrichment + end proxy support within 2 months** (CONFIRMED letter sent; CLAIMED contents); (4) **Iran denied receiving any new proposal** but Khamenei-advisor Shamkhani offered conditional acceptance on full sanctions removal; FM Araghchi told Tasnim enrichment 'not on the agenda' but 'addressed in later stages'; (5) **Hormuz transit per Iran's own claim ~30 vessels/day vs 140 pre-war** — Iran allowed 30+ Chinese ships since May 13 night under 'Iranian management protocols,' partially explaining the Aramco ~30/day benchmark; (6) **TTF Gas €49.86/MWh +4.63% to highest since April** = physical confirmation LNG complex feels the squeeze. Secondary contributor (~15%): refinery utilization seasonal ramp + RBOB rallying on summer driving prep ($3.70 +2.7% 1D, +17% 1M); EIA STEO May 12 confirmed $89 4Q26 / $79 2027 — official forecaster still below spot. **REVERSIBILITY ASYMMETRIC**: Iran has 2 months. If Shamkhani-Trump frame materializes (sanctions-for-dismantling), Brent gaps -$8-12 to $93-97 within 5 sessions. If Iran rejects formally and ceasefire collapses, Brent gaps +$8-15 toward StanChart $130-145 path. **MARKET REGIME SHIFT — three-force stagflation collision on May 15**: April PPI +1.4% m/m (biggest since Dec 2022, May 13 release) + Warsh Fed-Chair-transition (54-45 May 13, effective May 16) + Trump-Iran reversal = 10Y +14bp to 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025), 30Y +11bp to 5.121%, S&P -1.24%, NASDAQ -1.54%, Dow lost 50K, gold -1.83%, silver -10.6% one-day. **Crisis component of the day's S&P -1.2% is ~0.5%; rates/Fed is ~0.5%; sympathy with KOSPI/EM is ~0.2%.** Crisis component reverses if Iran responds within 2-month window; rates component does not. **EIA confirmed Hormuz crude/fuel flows dropped ~4 mbpd in March-April**; **Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial print WS408.13 = ~9x pre-war; Week 19 data not yet released through May 16 morning.** Goldman-JPM divergence persists ($85 vs $60). PRIOR (May 15): **C4 BRENT $107.42 / WTI $103.26 — REFUSING TO DEFLATE ON TRUMP-XI JOINT STATEMENT**: Brent +1.6% 1D / +8.1% 1M / +59.3% 3M; WTI +2.1% 1D / +9.1% 1M / +65.7% 3M. **Diplomatic words don't reopen shipping lanes that IRGC mines and small-boat attacks are physically closing.** The Trump-Xi joint statement that Hormuz 'must remain open' is the third repetition of this language since February — strait has remained largely blocked. Primary chain (crisis-linked, ~70%): Hormuz still physically closed at ~30 vessels/day vs 140 pre-war (Aramco CEO Nasser line unchanged); two new kinetic incidents May 14 (Hui Chuan seized off Fujairah, MSV Haj Ali sunk off Oman); Iran proactively cutting production because storage tanks are filling (blockade is binding); EIA May 13 weekly: crude -4.306 Mbbl draw vs -2.1 expected + Cushing -1.702 Mbbl + gasoline -4.084 Mbbl (13th straight); OPEC+ May 3 meeting confirmed only symbolic +206 kbd (most members physically cannot ship); Russian seaborne exports -24% in April from Ukrainian drone strikes; Druzhba flows to Germany halted May 1. Secondary contributor (~30%): refinery utilization +0.5pp to 90.7% (seasonal ramp), distillate slate shift; Trump 172M-barrel SPR emergency exchange actively executing (53.3 Mbbl awarded to Exxon/Trafigura/Marathon). **C5 RBOB GASOLINE -3.2% 1D IS NOT A DEMAND COLLAPSE — IT'S A REFINER SLATE UNWIND**: refiners shifting capacity from gasoline to distillates to chase Europe/Asia diesel-jet shortages; SPR exchange cushioning gasoline feedstock. RBOB-WTI divergence today (-3.2% vs +2.1%) = canary for refinery slate shift. **The paper-physical bifurcation is at cycle extreme**: Brent +1.6% refusing to fall on summit while VIX cycle-low 17.26, S&P/NASDAQ/Dow all at record closes, gold -2.2% (regime-shift unwind), aluminum -8.9% (premium unwind). **One side resolves violently within 5-10 sessions.** **Reversibility**: If Hormuz physically reopens with throughput >50 vessels/day sustained 3+ sessions, Brent could retrace $5-8 to $99-102. If joint statement produces no operational follow-up by May 20 and Brent breaks $108 sustained 2 sessions, StanChart $130-145 path activates. **VLCC TD3C NOW CONFIRMED AT WS459.28 / $467,408/day via Baltic Exchange Week 17** — ~4.2x late-January reading and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. Week 18 partial shows WS408.13 (Lloyd's: 'steepest one-day plunge in over half-decade') — still ~9x pre-war. **EIA May STEO: Brent ~$106 May/June 2026, $89 4Q26, $79 2027 average**; EIA-Goldman-JPM range ($79-$89-$60) = most divergent bulge-bracket-plus-official-forecaster spread of cycle. Heating Oil $3.95 (+1.2% 1D, +65.3% 3M); European TTF Gas ~€47/MWh +1.47%; AWRP ~1% (8x pre-war). **Trinary band ($100-110) has now held through SEVEN sessions of news escalation**; structural durability signal. PRIOR (May 14): Brent $105.68/$105.87 morning; $108-110 single-session breach not sustained; HEU-via-China door opened by Trump pre-summit. **A3 BRENT $105.68/105.87 MAY 14 MORNING (+0.0%/+0.22% 1D, +11.3% 1M, +56.7% vs Feb 28 pre-crisis ~$73)** — RETRACED from May 13 close $108.05 (single-session breach of $108-110 trigger zone). **$108-110 TRIGGER ZONE BREACH WAS SINGLE-SESSION, NOT SUSTAINED — the StanChart $130-145 cascade path therefore remains DORMANT** (per CRITICAL TAG-DISCIPLINE FLAG 2: trigger remains 'watching', NOT activated). The trinary band ($100-110) has now held through SIX sessions of news escalation (Trump rejection → Aramco 2-5 ships → Sit-Room → Litani → 90% rhetoric → Saudi-strikes-Reuters-amplify → Sledgehammer naming) = meaningful structural durability signal. **C1 PARADOX OF THE DAY: Brent REFUSING TO FALL despite Trump-Xi opening + crisis-trade unwind in defense/tankers/fertilizers** — Lockheed -15% MoM, ITA -4.1% MoM, CF Industries -3.8% 1D, Frontline -3.5% 1D, Cheniere -2.0% 1D, Aluminum -5.6%, RBOB -4.5%, VIX 17.87 (sub-18 on a 1-week-clock-day). **Paper says peace; Physical says blockade. One will resolve violently.** Why disconnect: Hormuz throughput physically at 2-5 ships/day (Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 line unchanged); **VLCC TD3C ~$420-462K/day per Lloyd's List May 11 'in unchartered territory'** (vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K — MAJOR ESCALATION SIGNAL); AWRP ~1% H&M / 7-day = 8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%, some transits paying up to 10%; TTF €46.63 May 13 (-0.11% 1D); helium 30% global supply disrupted; Aramco Q1 +25% via East-West reroute carries forward; ADNOC quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports (World Oil May 8). **B3 EIA MAY STEO PUBLISHED MAY 12 — Brent ~$106 May/June 2026, $89 4Q26, $79 2027 average** (CONFIRMED EIA primary; corrects yesterday's TASS-reported $94.85 = calendar-year 2026 average — both correct on different metrics). EIA: Iraq+Saudi+Kuwait+UAE+Qatar+Bahrain collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; assumption Hormuz effectively closed until late May, shipping picks up June. EIA $89 4Q26 cuts Goldman-JPM divergence ($85 vs $60) more by trimming Goldman than validating JPM. **EIA-Goldman-JPM range ($79-$89-$60) for late-2026/2027 is now the most divergent bulge-bracket-plus-official-forecaster spread of the cycle.** **BLOOMBERG MAY 13 SATELLITE: Iran's Kharg Island Oil Jetties Empty Again — second satellite snapshot of empty jetties this cycle, operational confirmation of Iranian export collapse without kinetic premium**. EIA additionally reported (Bloomberg May 13): Hormuz oil flows fell nearly 30% in Q1 2026 = official-data confirmation of Lloyd's List operational tracker series. **WTI $101.06 May 14 (+0.0% 1D, +10.7% 1M, +62.1% 3M); Brent-WTI spread $4.6 (compressed sustained).** Trump publicly opened HEU-via-China custodian door at Trump-Xi summit Day 1 (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — ToI May 14 liveblog primary) = NEW de-escalation pathway. Vance softer line. Pentagon Sledgehammer naming. Combined picture: Vance keeps diplomatic, Pentagon prepares kinetic, Trump positions in middle with HEU-via-China lever. Reversibility within band symmetric; asymmetric outside. Branch re-pricing: kinetic ~20-25% (DOWN — clock close without strike + Vance softer + HEU-via-China door); extended-economic-warfare ~52-57% (UP modestly — Sledgehammer + IRGC exercise bilateral kinetic-preparedness); negotiated-resolution ~22-27% (UP — China-custodian path explicitly opened). PRIOR (May 13): WTI $100.75 (-1.4% 1D). **THE $108-110 TRIGGER ZONE WAS TESTED AND FAILED — Brent settled at $106.29, BACK BELOW the threshold added to the graph May 12. StanChart $130-145 path therefore remains DORMANT.** The trinary band ($100-110) has now held through FIVE rounds of escalation (Trump rejection → Aramco 2-5 ships → Sit-Room weighing → Litani crossing → 90% rhetoric → Saudi-strikes disclosure) = structural durability, not a transient. Drivers of the retracement: (i) Trump-in-Beijing structural ceiling = summit makes kinetic strike order operationally impossible May 14-16; (ii) EIA cut 2026 Brent forecast to $94.85 (TASS) — official forecaster moved DOWN, pulled away from Goldman's $85 toward JPM's $60; (iii) Aramco Q1 +25% via East-West routing + 'quiet limited Hormuz crude exports resumed' (World Oil May 8) confirms physical supply ~90% preserved through East-West pipeline + UAE bypasses + quiet Hormuz transits — explains why Brent has not gone parabolic despite headline 96% non-Iranian Hormuz collapse; (iv) Iran missile fire rate -92% per JPost + CNN = capability vs willingness gap publicly quantified. **A2 APRIL CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS MAY 12) — three-year high; energy contributed >40% of headline gain (+17.9% YoY, gasoline +28.4% YoY)** — first cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through; closes near-term Fed easing door; US 10Y bid at 4.46% (+5bps); gold +0.8% only on real-yield channel competition. **GOLDMAN $85 vs JPM $60 = STILL $25/BBL DIVERGENCE; EIA $94.85 PULLED OFFICIAL-FORECASTER MEDIAN DOWN — spot $40+ above JPM's 2026 average means market NOT pricing JPM $60 case; either JPM decisively wrong or market in fear-premium regime that reverses on genuine de-escalation.** Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 'market won't normalise until 2027 if Hormuz disruption persists past mid-June' (CNBC primary); Q1 +25% profit + East-West pipeline pushed to 7 MMbpd capacity with Yanbu exports 3.66-4 MMbpd; ADNOC quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports (World Oil May 8). **EIA also confirmed non-Iranian Hormuz crude exports avg ~500,000 bpd since early March vs ~13.6 MMbpd pre-war (-96%).** PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION DEEPENED: physical (transit collapsed) signals extended disruption; paper-Brent hesitating at $108 threshold partly because of compensating Saudi/UAE non-Hormuz routing + Trump-Xi summit forced pause. Reversibility WITHIN BAND symmetric; asymmetric outside: upside break >$110 sustained 2 sessions = StanChart $130-145 path activates (cascade to inr-usd, nifty-50, sp-500, marine-war-risk-insurance); downside break <$95 sustained 3+ sessions = peace-dividend thesis re-emerges, CF/Nutrien fertilizer trade reverses violently. **TTF Gas ~€46/MWh range (May 11 last live print €46.23, flat-to-slight up into May 13 with no fresh European Council action); AWRP unchanged ~1% (8x pre-war); VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day, Frontline (+1.0% 1D, +10.6% 1M, +28.6% 3M) — insurers + tanker market have NOT bought de-escalation.** Branch read remains: kinetic ~22-27%, extended-economic-warfare ~50-55%, negotiated-resolution ~18-23%. **TRINARY-REGIME HARD FLOOR ESTABLISHED AT $100-105 THROUGH FIVE SESSIONS OF NEWS ESCALATION.** Brent has been pushed up by: Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal ('TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE') + WH Sit-Room weighing renewed strikes + Hormuz transit DETERIORATED to 2-5 ships/day per Aramco CEO Nasser (vs ~6/day May 10) + Goldman Sachs raised 2026 average forecast to **$85/bbl from $77** (+$8) on extended-Hormuz-disruption thesis + VLCC Agios Fanourios I U-turn confidence-collapse signature + new OFAC sanctions wave (12 entities + 3 individuals, Iran→China oil) + second Kharg slick May 11-12 + UAE-Lavan revelation (April 8 strike confirmed retroactive). **GOLDMAN VS JPM $25/BBL 2026 AVERAGE GAP IS THE LARGEST BULGE-BRACKET DIVERGENCE OF CYCLE:** Goldman $85 (with Q2 trimmed to $90 on prior ceasefire optics now stale, Q4 base $71); JPM stays $60 (Natasha Kaneva — bearish base case, 'oil surplus visible in January, likely to persist,' expects voluntary + involuntary production cuts to clear surplus, argues markets are $10/bbl above fair value on Iran fear premium). Spot is $40+ above JPM's 2026 average — market NOT pricing JPM $60 case; either JPM decisively wrong OR market is in fear-premium regime that reverses on any genuine de-escalation. **TTF Gas €46.23 May 11 (+4.74% d/d, +5% from May 8 €44 baseline).** AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day, 8x pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline; VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day, Frontline +11.1% 1M / +32.6% 3M though Frontline -3.4% 1D today on Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply. **BRENT $108-110 BREAK WOULD ACTIVATE STANCHART $130-145 PATH.** Trump China summit May 14-15 forces pause window over 1-week clock close. If Trump returns from Beijing May 15-16 without ordering strikes, Brent could retrace $5-8 toward $97-100; if he orders strikes, Brent +$8-15 within 24h toward StanChart path. **PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION DEEPENED**: physical (Hormuz throughput, AWRP, VLCC rates) all signal extended disruption; paper futures still in oscillating trinary band reflecting Goldman-JPM divergence.

Below $108 close = trinary $100-110 band holds against NYT preparation report + Trump 'Calm Before the Storm' (strongest escalation signal of cycle).
current.branch_status: Branch 1 (kinetic) ~25-30% (UP — Trump immediate post-summit reversal + new May 16 nuclear ultimatum letter); Branch 2 (extended-economic-warfare/trinary) ~50-55% (HOLDS — Hormuz physically constrained, Iran-Chinese selective transit now operational, OPEC+ shut-in capacity); Branch 3 (de-escalation) ~20-25% (DOWN — joint statement produced zero operational follow-through; HEU-custodian door operationally empty).
current.regime_shift_marker: May 15-16 — StanChart $130-145 path moves DORMANT → WATCHING-ACTIVE. Brent's $108-110 trigger zone was breached single-session May 13 ($108.05), retraced May 14 ($105.68), now CLOSED ABOVE $108 May 15 ($109.26) = first 2-session sustain since trigger was added. The paper-physical bifurcation that peaked May 14 has RESOLVED VIOLENTLY into physical confirmation: paper-Brent agrees with physical that blockade is binding. Three-force collision on May 15 (PPI shock + Warsh + Trump reversal) set the inflation regime: 10Y +14bp to 4.595%, 30Y +11bp to 5.121%, S&P -1.24%, silver -10.6%, gold -1.83%. EIA $89 4Q26 / $79 2027 anchor still below spot — official forecaster bullish-incompatible with current tape.

current.paper_physical_bifurcation: RESOLVED toward physical confirmation. Brent closed $109.26 — paper now agrees with physical that blockade is binding. Physical: Hormuz throughput ~30/day per Aramco (partially explained by Iran-Chinese selective transit); VLCC TD3C Week 18 partial WS408.13 (~9x pre-war); AWRP ~1% (8x pre-war); EIA Hormuz Q1 -30%. Paper: WTI +11% weekly = cleanest week of physical-tightness reflection in cycle.

active_triggers.failure_note: Brent broke above $105 on May 12 ($105.58) following Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal; peace-dividend thesis falsified for this window.

active_triggers.condition: Brent breaks $108-110 sustained 2 sessions — would activate StanChart $130-145 path
active_triggers.note_2026-05-14: May 13 close $108.05 = SINGLE-SESSION BREACH. May 14 morning retraced to $105.68/$105.87. 2-session sustain rule UNMET — trigger remains watching, NOT activated. StanChart $130-145 path remains DORMANT.
active_triggers.fact_check_correction_2026-05-16: Today's brief draft moved this trigger to active_fired on the basis of 'May 13 close $108.05 + May 15 close $109.26 = 2-session sustain MET.' Verification gate reverted this back to WATCHING. yfinance closes (front-month ICE Brent BZ=F): May 12 $107.77, May 13 $105.63, May 14 $105.72, May 15 $109.26. Earlier prints of '$108.05' for May 13 appear to be intraday (May 13 high was $108.31), not close. Only May 15 is a confirmed close above $108. The 2-session-of-closes sustain rule is NOT met. StanChart $130-145 path stays DORMANT. Monday May 18 close is the next test — if it closes above $108, the rule is met (under either consecutive-close or 2-closes interpretation).

active_triggers.condition: VLCC TD3C tanker rate sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions — paper-physical divergence terminal escalation
active_triggers.note: Lloyd's List May 11 reports VLCC TD3C 'in unchartered territory' at $420-462K/day vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K (markets dossier May 13). MAJOR escalation signal — paper-Brent at $105 vs spot VLCC at $420K is the cleanest paper-physical divergence indicator we have. If sustained 5+ sessions, terminal escalation of physical-disruption regime. Source verification: Lloyd's List primary + Baltic Exchange data. Trigger added at ACTIVE not WATCHING because the $420K figure is operationally massive and 4x the prior recorded peak; if Lloyd's $420K is accurate, the threshold is already breached. Tomorrow's pipeline should explicitly cross-check Baltic Exchange WS prints for verification. | 2026-05-14 fact-check: demoted from active → watching. Basis is single Lloyd's List May 11 print at $420-462K/day pending Baltic Exchange WS cross-verification; the 5-session-sustain requirement is not established from a single data point.

active_triggers.condition: Brent gaps -$8-12 to $93-97 within 5 sessions on Trump-Xi deliverable
active_triggers.note: Markets dossier C1 scenario A: Paper proven right — Trump-Xi delivers HEU-custodian framework, MOU signs by May 17-20, Hormuz transit ramps back, Brent gaps -$8-12. If this trigger fires, crisis-trade unwind completes (defense bid further, gold under more pressure, India equities rally toward Nifty 24,500-25,000).

active_triggers.condition: Brent spikes above $130 on Houthi Red Sea entry

EIA $89 4Q26 cuts Goldman-JPM divergence ($85 vs $60) more by trimming Goldman than validating JPM. **EIA-Goldman-JPM range ($79-$89-$60) for late-2026/2027 = most divergent bulge-bracket-plus-official-forecaster spread of cycle.** WTI $101.06 May 14 (+0.0% 1D, +10.7% 1M, +62.1% 3M); Brent-WTI spread $4.6 (compressed sustained). **Trump publicly opened HEU-via-China custodian door** at Trump-Xi summit Day 1 (CONFIRMED RHETORIC ToI May 14 liveblog primary) = NEW de-escalation pathway in different jurisdiction. recent_signals.verification: Brent $105.68/$105.87 May 14 morning = CONFIRMED tape (Trading Economics + yfinance); May 13 close $108.05 single-session breach = CONFIRMED tape; EIA May STEO = CONFIRMED (EIA primary); Bloomberg Kharg jetties + Hormuz Q1 -30% = CONFIRMED (Bloomberg + EIA primary); VLCC TD3C $420-462K/day = REPORTED (Lloyd's List primary; figure is operationally large — flagged for cross-verification with Baltic Exchange); Trump HEU-via-China statement = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (ToI primary).

recent_signals.headline: BRENT $106.29 MAY 13 (-1.4% 1D, +12.1% 1M, +56.9% vs Feb 28 ~$73) AFTER INTRADAY $107.05 PRINT (implied May 12 close ~$107.77 = +2% rally on Trump-rejection + April CPI energy + Aramco 2-5 ships/day + Saudi-strikes disclosure). $108-110 TRIGGER ZONE TESTED AND FAILED — Brent BACK BELOW threshold added to graph May 12. StanChart $130-145 path remains DORMANT. WTI $100.75 (-1.4% 1D), spread ~$5.5. Drivers of retracement: Trump-in-Beijing summit-pause ceiling (May 14-16 = kinetic strike order operationally impossible); EIA cut 2026 forecast to $94.85 (TASS, official forecaster moving DOWN, away from Goldman $85); Aramco Q1 +25% on East-West rerouting + Yanbu exports 3.66-4 MMbpd through mid-May + ADNOC quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports (World Oil May 8); Iran missile fire rate -92% (JPost + CNN intel) = capability ≠ willingness ≠ logistics. **April CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS May 12, three-year high since May 2023, +0.6% MoM; CORE +2.8% YoY / +0.4% MoM; energy contributed >40% of headline gain, +17.9% YoY; gasoline +28.4% YoY; shelter +0.6%; BLS itself flagged inflation 'a problem beyond Iran war impacts')** — first cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through; closes near-term Fed easing door; US 10Y bid at 4.46% (+5bps). Trinary band $100-110 has now held through FIVE rounds of escalation (Trump rejection May 11 → Aramco 2-5 ships → Sit-Room weighing → Litani crossing → 90% rhetoric → Saudi-strikes disclosure) = structural durability not transient. Spot $40+ above JPM's $60 2026 average = either JPM decisively wrong or market in fear-premium regime that reverses only on genuine de-escalation. Paper-physical bifurcation deepened: EIA confirmed non-Iranian Hormuz crude exports avg ~500,000 bpd since early March vs ~13.6 MMbpd pre-war (-96%). VIX FELL to 17.99 (-2.1% 1D) — markets pricing Trump-in-Beijing = forced kinetic pause; gold +0.8% only (real-yield channel competing with safe-haven bid). Goldman-JPM $25/bbl 2026 average gap intact; EIA $94.85 pulled official-forecaster median DOWN. Reversibility: upside break >$110 sustained 2 sessions = StanChart $130-145 activates; downside break <$95 sustained 3+ sessions = peace-dividend re-emerges.

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY (US, India, most W Europe markets CLOSED) — FRIDAY MAY 8 CLOSE $101.29-$101.73 STILL OPERATIVE. recent_signals.headline: BRENT CLOSE $101.73 MAY 8 (+1.66% 1D); FRIDAY RANGE $96.07 → $103.96 = TRINARY-REGIME SIGNATURE OSCILLATION; SUB-$105 CHAIN DAY 3 INTACT BUT OSCILLATING. The Friday tape low → high → close sequence ($96 → $104 → $101) is the cleanest signature of a market not believing either binary side (deal signs OR full kinetic) and pricing the trinary middle. The $96 intraday low reflects MOU-progress hopes (May 7); the $104 May 8 intraday high reflects second day of US tanker strikes (M/T Sea Star III, M/T Sevda) + CENTCOM disclosure of 70-tanker blockade scale + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' Hormuz speech. Goldman Q2 $90 target now stretched against Lloyd's List zero-transit print May 7 + 1,500-vessel Persian Gulf standstill — likely revises higher if trinary regime extends past May 21. Standard Chartered $130-145 path remains conditional on MOU collapse. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day, 6-8x pre-war norm — insurers have NOT bought peace story; physical-paper bifurcation deepened.
sources: Goldman Sachs Q2 research note (institutional anchor)
